---
title: ""
image: "../../images/py_kurs_cover03.png"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
page-layout: full
---

<br>

::: {.d-flex .gap-2}
[🏠 Home](../../index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[🐍 Python Overview](../index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
:::

# Week 3: Python Basics III
*The foundation of your Python journey.*

::: {layout-ncol=1}
### (Fundamentals)
* [ **Python Conditions**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_conditions.asp)
* [ **Python While Loops**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_while_loops.asp)
* [ **Python For Loops**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_for_loops.asp)
* [ **Python Functions**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_functions.asp)
* [ **Python Lambda**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_lambda.asp)
::: 

<br>

## Project 3: Random Number Guesser

::: {.panel-tabset}

### 📝 Die Aufgabe
Your task is to code the number guesser game. You should generate a random number and the user has to guess until they guess the number.

ToDo's:

Initialize a random number
Create a loop which should loop until the number is guessed
Ask the user for their guess
If the guessed number equals the actual number then print "Hurraaa you won! GUESSED_NUMBER was the corret guess."
If the guessed number is larger than the actual number then print "Go lower!"
If the guessed number is smaller than the actual number then print "Go higher!"
Optional:

You can implement lives so the user has to guess the guess number within given lives
Give the user the chance to play again if they guessed the correct number

### 💡 Die Lösung
```python
import random

num = random.randrange(0,100)

number_guessed = False

while not number_guessed:
    guess = int(input("Guess a number between 0 and 100: "))
    if guess == num:
        print(f"Hurraaa you won! {guess} was the corret guess.")
        play_again = input("Do you want to play again? (y/n): ")
        if play_again == "y": 
            num = random.randrange(0,100)
        else:
            number_guessed = True
    elif guess > num:
        print("Go lower!")
    else:
        print("Go higher!")
```

:::

## Material

::: {layout-ncol=2}
[GitHub Repo](https://www.github.com/STADS-Mannheim/python-course-2025/tree/main){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg .w-100}

[w3schools Tutorial](https://www.w3schools.com/python/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg .w-100}
:::
